threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You carry a reusable coffee cup but haven’t figured out the local cafes all serve cold brew – only the hip ones offer a decent hot latte.
Last night on the 59, a guy tried to barter a can of Fanta for a seat. The driver just shook his head, but you could see the humour in everyone’s eyes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple of kids selling lemonade on Schillerpromenade, I realized this isn't just a place—it's a community.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you stumble into a hidden U-Bahn station, complete with vintage ads and an echo of the past, if you know where to look.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they navigate the Civic Center chaos—while newbies just freeze at the BART detour signs.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Nothing like the chaos of a Muni delay due to a dog on the tracks while someone tries to sell you a mixtape through the window.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think the fountains in Plaça de la Vila are for show, but they're actually a hit spot for locals to cool down and meet up on hot days.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their reluctance to try the caña—a local always knows it’s the small glass, not the pint.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest tube station without realizing none are close enough to bother.
Heard a bloke on the 59 last night claiming he could predict the future based on the bus routes. Then he pointed out the wrong stop twice.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When the kid at the Kiosk knew my order before I walked in. That felt like home, like I was part of the neighborhood's rhythm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a club that operates in a former factory, complete with a swimming pool. It's wild and feels like stepping into a time capsule.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour, especially with all the recent delays and the dog's still-on-the-tracks antics.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a couple arguing loudly on the bus, mid-sentence he stopped and handed her a slice of pizza. Only in SF do disputes come with a side of carbs.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for selfies; they miss the real vibe in the quiet backstreets, where locals sip coffee and debate fútbol.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they stare at the metro map like it’s a treasure map, trying to decipher the lines and stops.
